steps.title: Identify what the data must support
steps.content: The sentence reports a research institute's finding about the share of congressional members with past military experience. The completion must be an accurate statement that the graph actually shows.

steps.title: Match a well-documented historical trend
steps.content: The percentage of veterans in both the House and Senate fell sharply between the 1970s and the 2010s as the World War II and Korean War generations aged out of Congress, dropping from roughly three quarters of each chamber to under one fifth. A 1973-to-2013 substantial decrease in both houses fits this established pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the House had a higher percentage than the Senate from 2003 to 2023': across this span the two chambers track closely, so a clean higher-vs-lower claim misreads the comparison. ✗
  • Choice B — 'those members constituted a majority in both houses from 1953 to 2003': veterans fell below 50 percent well before 2003, so this overstates how late the majority lasted. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the percentage decreased substantially in both houses from 1973 to 2013': matches the steep downward slopes in both lines across that 40-year window. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the percentage remained fairly consistent from 1993 to 2023': the share kept dropping through these decades, so "fairly consistent" contradicts the trend. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
